title: helianthemum glaucum var. croceum
intro: Helianthemum glaucum var. croceum, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species within the family Cistaceae, characterized by its pink to yellow flowers and drought-tolerant nature. Traditionally, rockrose has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activities, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, reduced fever, and improved respiratory function. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to harness its therapeutic potential.
